commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a gateway fallback that appends one display-safe assistant error transcript entry for started agent runs that return error payloads, plus a runtime-persistence signal to avoid duplicate error turns.

PR surface: Source +40, Tests +79. Total +119 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main has a started-agent returned-error branch that broadcasts the error without its own assistant transcript append. The PR also reports failing regression expectations on unpatched main, but I did not run a live provider-outage reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is indicated; the maintainer decision is whether to require contributor real behavior proof or accept the current test-only evidence before merge.

target.title: [AI-assisted] Persist agent-run error replies in chat history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: When an agent run returned Gateway-delivered error payloads after the run had started, the live UI saw an error broadcast but the assistant error was not durably appended to the chat transcript.
- Solution: Persist exactly one display-safe assistant error through the existing transcript append path before broadcasting the returned agent error, unless the runtime already persisted an assistant error.
- What changed: The gateway now tracks runtime-owned assistant-error persistence, forwards that signal through auto-reply runners, appends a deduped assistant error on the Gateway fallback path, and locks the behavior with regression tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No raw JSONL writes, no provider behavior changes, no delivery routing changes, no dependency changes, and no lifecycle or config changes.

## Motivation

- Control UI and `chat.history` should stay consistent after an agent-run failure. A visible error reply should not disappear from durable history just because the failure came through the returned-agent-error payload path.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #89197
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Agent-run error payloads are now represented in durable chat history as exactly one assistant error message.
- Real environment tested: Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout on fresh `origin/main` `00d846da`, dependencies installed with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, tests run through repository scripts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
git apply ../patch.diff
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -- -t 'assistant error'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.error-broadcast.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t 'assistant error'
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Focused Gateway assistant-error proof: 2 passed, 103 skipped.
Adjacent Gateway proof: 107 passed.
Session tool result guard proof: 35 passed.
Auto-reply assistant-error proof: 3 passed, 219 skipped.
Typecheck: node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json completed successfully.
git diff --check completed successfully.
```

- Observed result after fix: The started-agent error path appends one assistant transcript message with idempotency key `<runId>:assistant-error`, keeps the existing error broadcast, and skips the fallback append when runtime persistence already happened.
- What was not tested: A live provider outage with real credentials and a Control UI screenshot were not run. The proof uses the real Gateway/server-method test path with synthetic agent-run error payloads.
- Before evidence:

```text
Unpatched fresh origin/main with the final regression expectations failed as expected:
- broadcasts returned agent-run error payloads after an agent starts: expected [] to have a length of 1.
- persists one assistant error when a started agent returns an error before runtime persistence: expected [] to have a length of 1.
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `chat.send` handled returned agent error payloads by broadcasting an error terminal event, but that branch did not perform its own assistant transcript append.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered the visible error broadcast and user transcript fallback, but did not assert that the assistant error becomes durable in chat history.
- Contributing context (if known): Runtime fallback candidates can already persist assistant error stubs; the Gateway fallback needed a duplicate-prevention signal so the repair would not create two assistant error turns.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: A started agent returns an error payload before runtime-owned assistant persistence; Gateway appends one assistant error to the transcript before broadcasting the error.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It exercises the Gateway `chat.send` path, transcript update emission, idempotency key, and duplicate guard without requiring live provider credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing broadcast coverage was present but only asserted the live error response, not durable assistant transcript state.
- If no new test is added, why not: New regression tests were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users should keep an error assistant turn in chat history after a started agent run fails before normal runtime persistence, instead of seeing the live error while later history appears incomplete.

## Diagram

```text
agent error payload
  -> Gateway returnedAgentErrorPayloads branch
  -> if runtime did not persist assistant error: append assistant transcript message with <runId>:assistant-error
  -> broadcast existing chat error terminal event
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No new access scope; the change writes a display-safe assistant error into the same session transcript path already used by Gateway assistant persistence.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable testserver checkout
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw repository scripts with pnpm-managed dependencies
- Model/provider: Synthetic agent-run error payload; no live provider credentials
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway chat/server-method path
- Relevant config (redacted): Default test fixture config; no secrets used

### Steps

1. Apply only the regression-test portion of the patch to unpatched `origin/main` and run the assistant-error focused Gateway tests.
2. Confirm unpatched `origin/main` fails because no assistant error is persisted.
3. Apply the full patch.
4. Run focused Gateway assistant-error tests.
5. Run adjacent Gateway/session guard tests, typecheck, and auto-reply assistant-error tests.

### Expected

- Unpatched source fails the new assistant-error transcript expectations.
- Patched source persists one assistant error and keeps duplicate prevention when runtime persistence already happened.

### Actual

- Before: two assistant-error transcript expectations failed because the assistant error list was empty.
- After: focused, adjacent, typecheck, and extra auto-reply proof all passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
Before: 2 failed assistant-error transcript expectations on unpatched fresh origin/main.
After: focused Gateway assistant-error proof 2/2 passed.
After: adjacent Gateway proof 107/107 passed.
After: session-tool-result guard proof 35/35 passed.
After: auto-reply assistant-error proof 3/3 passed.
After: tsgo core typecheck passed.
```

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Missing Gateway assistant-error transcript append, existing returned-agent-error broadcast behavior, duplicate prevention when runtime already persisted the assistant error, adjacent chat error broadcast behavior, session tool result guard behavior, and auto-reply error persistence callback forwarding.
- Edge cases checked: Runtime-owned assistant error persistence does not produce a second Gateway fallback assistant error; Gateway fallback assistant error uses a stable `<runId>:assistant-error` idempotency key.
- What you did **not** verify: Live provider outage, real Control UI screenshot/recording, and full repository-wide test suite.

## Review Conversations

- [x] No PR review conversations exist yet.
- [x] Issue-side ClawSweeper guidance was addressed in the patch shape and proof plan; no GitHub reply or bot command was posted.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Not applicable.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The Gateway fallback could duplicate an assistant error that runtime already persisted.
  - Mitigation: Auto-reply runners now forward the runtime assistant-error persistence signal, and Gateway skips its fallback append once that signal is observed.
- Risk: Error text could bypass normal assistant display sanitization.
  - Mitigation: The fallback message uses `sanitizeAssistantDisplayText` before transcript append.
- Risk: Dependency Guard could require security approval.
  - Mitigation: No dependency manifests, lockfiles, or dependency blocks were changed.
